dashboardItems.text:
*DEFINITIONS*
____________________


*HYPERTENSION TREATMENT OUTCOMES*

*Controlled BP at latest visit in past 3 months*

_Numerator_: Patients with controlled blood pressure (<140 systolic AND <90 diastolic) recorded at the patient's most recent visit within the past 3 months.

_Denominator_: Patients under care in the hypertension program registered before the past 3 months. Exclusions: Dead and 12 month lost to follow-up patients are excluded from the denominator.

*Uncontrolled BP at latest visit in past 3 months*

_Numerator_: Patients with uncontrolled blood pressure (≥140 systolic OR ≥90 diastolic) recorded at the patient's most recent visit within the past 3 months

_Denominator_: Patients under care in the hypertension program registered before the past 3 months. Exclusions: Dead and 12 month lost to follow-up patients are excluded from the denominator.

*No visit in past 3 months*

_Numerator_: Patients with no BP measure recorded in the past 3 months.

_Denominator_: Patients under care in the hypertension program registered before the past 3 months. Exclusions: Dead and 12 month lost to follow-up patients are excluded from the denominator.

*Q: Why are patients registered within the past 3 months excluded from treatment outcome figures?*

Three months gives patients time to take their hypertension medication and to get their blood pressure controlled. Most newly registered patients have uncontrolled blood pressure and including them would not reflect an accurate picture of actual controlled patients.



*REGISTRATIONS*

*Patients under care*

Cumulative registrations in the hypertension program excluding 12 month lost to follow-up and Dead patients.

*Cumulative registrations*

Cumulative registrations in the hypertension program excluding Dead patients.

*Monthly registrations*

New patients registered in the hypertension program in a month.



*12 MONTH LOST TO FOLLOW UP*

*12 month lost to follow-up*

_Numerator_: Patients with no BP measure recorded in the past 12 months.

_Denominator_: Cumulative registrations in the hypertension program excluding Dead patients



*HYPERTENSION TREATMENT CASCADE*

*% of people registered*

_Numerator_: Cumulative registrations

_Denominator_: Estimated number of adults with hypertension in the community.

*% of people under care*

_Numerator_: Patients under care

_Denominator_: Estimated number of adults with hypertension in the community.

*% of people with controlled BP*

_Numerator_: Patients with controlled blood pressure in the past 3 months.

_Denominator_: Estimated number of adults with hypertension in the community.



*COHORT REPORTS*

_BP controlled numerator_: Patients with a BP (<140 systolic AND <90 diastolic) at their last visit in the quarter after the quarter when they were registered.

_BP not controlled numerator_: Patients with a BP (≥140 systolic OR ≥90 diastolic) at their last visit in the quarter after the quarter when they were registered.

_No visit numerator_: Patients with no visit in the quarter after the quarter when they were registered.

_Denominator_: New patients registered in the hypertension program in a quarter.

Each quarterly cohort report refers to outcomes for patients registered in the previous quarter.
For example, the Apr-Jun quarterly cohort refers to treatment outcomes for patients registered in Jan-Mar.

Each quarterly cohort report refers to outcomes for patients registered in the previous quarter.
For example, the Apr-Jun quarterly cohort refers to treatment outcomes for patients registered in Jan-Mar.

*DEFINITIONS*
____________________


*DIABETES TREATMENT OUTCOMES*

*Controlled blood sugar at latest visit in past 3 months*

_Numerator_: Patients with controlled blood sugar (FBS <126 mg/dL or HbA1c <7%) recorded at the patient's most recent visit within the past 3 months.

_Denominator_: Patients under care in the diabetes program registered before the past 3 months. Exclusions: Dead and 12 month lost to follow-up patients are excluded from the denominator.

*Uncontrolled blood sugar at latest visit in past 3 months*

_Numerator_: Patients with uncontrolled blood sugar (FBS ≥126 mg/dL or HbA1c ≥7%) recorded at the patient's most recent visit within the past 3 months

_Denominator_: Patients under care in the diabetes program registered before the past 3 months. Exclusions: Dead and 12 month lost to follow-up patients are excluded from the denominator.

*No visit in past 3 months*

_Numerator_: Patients with no blood sugar measure recorded in the past 3 months.

_Denominator_: Patients under care in the diabetes program registered before the past 3 months. Exclusions: Dead and 12 month lost to follow-up patients are excluded from the denominator.

*Q: Why are patients registered within the past 3 months excluded from treatment outcome figures?*

Three months gives patients time to take their diabetes medication and to get their blood sugar controlled. Most newly registered patients have uncontrolled blood sugar and including them would not reflect an accurate picture of actual controlled patients.



*REGISTRATIONS*

*Patients under care*

Cumulative registrations in the diabetes program excluding 12 month lost to follow-up and Dead patients.

*Cumulative registrations*

Cumulative registrations in the diabetes program excluding Dead patients.

*Monthly registrations*

New patients registered in the diabetes program in a month.



*12 MONTH LOST TO FOLLOW UP*

*12 month lost to follow-up*

_Numerator_: Patients with no blood sugar measure recorded in the past 12 months.

_Denominator_: Cumulative registrations in the diabetes program excluding Dead patients



*DIABETES TREATMENT CASCADE*

*Estimated people with diabetes*

Estimated adults over 30 with diabetes reported from STEPS Community Survey

*% of people registered*

_Numerator_: Cumulative registrations

_Denominator_: Estimated people with diabetes.

*% of people under care*

_Numerator_: Patients under care 

_Denominator_: Estimated people with diabetes.

*% of people with controlled BP*

_Numerator_: Patients with controlled blood sugar (FBS <126 mg/dL or HbA1c <7%) recorded at the patient's most recent visit within the past 3 months registered before that past 3 months.

_Denominator_: Estimated people with diabetes.



*COHORT REPORTS*

_Controlled blood sugar numerator_: The number of patients with a FBS <126 mg/dL or HbA1c <7% at their last visit in the quarter after the quarter when they were registered.

_Uncontrolled blood sugar numerator_: The number of patients with a FBS ≥126 mg/dL or HbA1c ≥7% at their last visit in the quarter after the quarter when they were registered.

_No visit numerator_: The number of patients with no visit in the quarter after the quarter when they were registered.

_Denominator_: New patients registered in the diabetes program in a quarter.

dashboards.name: 2. Diabetes Program

dashboardItems.text:
*DEFINITIONS*
____________________


*OVERDUE PATIENTS*

*Overdue patient*

A patient with a scheduled visit date which has passed with no visit.

*% Overdue patients*

Numerator: Contactable hypertension patients that are overdue at the start of the month. Patients with no phone number, and transferred patients are excluded.

Denominator: Patients under care. 12 month lost to follow-up and dead patients are excluded.

*Q: Patients become overdue during the month, why do we only patients that are overdue before the 1st of the month?*

The number of overdue patients will change daily, making it difficult to manage. Using the 1st of the month as a cut-off creates a fixed value for the month.


*Q: What is a transferred patient*

A patient is marked as 'transferred' if they have a call with result 'Remove from overdue' with one of the following reasons selected:
- 'Moved to a private practitioner'
- 'Transferred to another primary health facility'
- 'Moved' (left the area)



*OVERDUE PATIENTS CALLED*

*% Overdue patients called*

_Numerator_: Unique hypertension patients with at least one calling report event during the month. 12 month lost to follow-up, transferred and dead patients are excluded.

_Note: This number includes patients that become overdue during the month and received a call, they may not be part of the denominator._

_Denominator_: Contactable hypertension patients that are overdue at the start of the month. Patients with no phone number, and transferred patients are excluded.

*% Overdue patients called by call result*

*- % Overdue patients called during the month with result ‘Agreed to visit’*

_Numerator_: Patients with call result 'Agreed to visit' of their first call during the month.

_Denominator_: Unique hypertension patients with at least one call during the month. 12 month lost to follow-up, transferred and dead patients are excluded.

*- % Overdue patients called during the month with result ‘Remind to call later’*

_Numerator_: Patients with call result 'Remind to call later' of their first call during the month.

_Denominator_: Unique hypertension patients with at least one call during the month. 12 month lost to follow-up, transferred and dead patients are excluded.

*- % Overdue patients called during the month with result ‘Remove from overdue’*

_Numerator_: Patients with call result 'Remove from overdue' of their first call during the month.

_Denominator_: Unique hypertension patients with at least one call during the month. 12 month lost to follow-up, transferred and dead patients are excluded.

*- % Overdue patients called during the month with no result recorded*

_Numerator_: Patients with call no result recorded of their first call during the month.

_Denominator_: Unique hypertension patients with at least one call during the month.12 month lost to follow-up, transferred and dead patients are excluded.

*Q: What does ‘no result recorded’ mean?*

A user has created a calling event but did not enter the ‘Result of call’ information before completing.



*OVERDUE PATIENTS THAT RETURNED TO CARE*

*% Called overdue patients that returned to care*

_Numerator_: Hypertension patients that returned to care within 15 days of their first call during the month.

_Denominator_: Hypertension patients with at least one call during the month. 12 month lost to follow-up, transferred and dead patients are excluded.


*% Called overdue patients that returned to care by call result*

*- % Called overdue patients with result ‘Agreed to visit’ that returned within 15 days*

Numerator: Patients that returned to care within 15 days

Denominator: Hypertension patients with call result 'Agreed to visit' of their first call during the month. 12 month lost to follow-up, transferred and dead patients are excluded.

*- % Called overdue patients with result ‘Remind to call later’ that returned within 15 days*

Numerator: Patients that returned to care within 15 days

Denominator: Hypertension patients with call result 'Remind to call later' of their first call during the month. 12 month lost to follow-up, transferred and dead patients are excluded.

*- Called overdue patients with result ‘Remove from overdue’ that returned within 15 days*

Numerator: Patients that returned to care within 15 days

Denominator: Hypertension patients with call result 'Remove from overdue' of their first call during the month. 12 month lost to follow-up, transferred and dead patients are excluded.

*- % Called overdue patients with no result recorded that returned within 15 days*

Numerator: Patients that returned to care within 15 days

Denominator: Hypertension patients with no call result recorded of their first call during the month. 12 month lost to follow-up, transferred and dead patients are excluded.

A{v4DnYfXn9Mu} == 'YES' && A{fI1P3Mg1zOZ} != 'DIED' && d2:monthsBetween(PS_EVENTDATE:anb2cjLx3WM,V{analytics_period_end}) < 3 
&& if(#{anb2cjLx3WM.cE3KOFmoYbZ} == 'HBA1C',

programRules.condition: d2:hasValue(A{dob})
programRules.name: Age (For TEI header)

programRules.condition: !d2:hasValue(#{cholesterol_mgdL}) || #{cholesterol_mgdL} == '' || #{cholesterol_mgdL} == null
programRules.description: If total cholesterol is deleted, we need to also delete the calculation
programRules.name: ASSIGN Cleanup Total Cholesterol in mmol/L if mg/dL value is removed

programRules.condition: d2:hasValue(#{cholesterol_mgdL})
programRules.description: The WHO charts are in mmol/L but data entry is made in mg/dL
programRules.name: ASSIGN Convert Total Cholesterol from mg/dL to mmol/L

programRules.condition: d2:hasValue(A{weight_tea}) && !d2:hasValue(#{previousWeight})
programRules.name: ASSIGN enrollment weight if no previous visit

programRules.condition: d2:hasValue(A{height_tea}) && A{height_tea} != 0
programRules.name: ASSIGN Height from TEA to DE

programRules.condition: d2:hasValue(#{usePrevWeight}) && #{usePrevWeight} != null && #{usePrevWeight} != 0 && #{usePrevWeight} == true
programRules.name: ASSIGN weight from previous visit

programRules.condition: true
programRules.name: AUTOFILL Patient status to "ACTIVE"

programRules.condition: d2:hasValue(#{Weight}) && #{Weight} > 0 && d2:hasValue(#{Height}) && #{Height} > 0
programRules.description: If Weight and Height are input then calculate the BMI
programRules.name: BMI_Calculated

programRules.condition: #{BloodSugarTypeUnits}==true
programRules.name: Convert blood sugar unit codes to readable text

programRules.condition: #{BloodSugarTypeOption} == ''
programRules.description: This rule hides the unit field and all the numeric fields related to blood sugar till user chooses the type of blood sugar measure.
programRules.name: HIDE: All blood sugar fields till type of measure is chosen

programRules.condition: !d2:hasValue(A{patientCalledStatus}) || (A{patientCalledStatus} != 'OVERDUE_PENDING_CALL' && A{patientCalledStatus} != 'CALLED')
programRules.description: Hide calling report program stage if patient is not overdue
programRules.name: HIDE: Calling report if patient is NOT overdue

programRules.condition: !d2:hasValue(A{HTNdiagnosis}) || A{HTNdiagnosis}== 'NO'
programRules.name: HIDE: Did patient take BP medicines any time in the last month

programRules.condition: !d2:hasValue(A{DMdiagnosis}) || A{DMdiagnosis}== 'NO'
programRules.name: HIDE: Did patient take diabetes medicines any time in the last month

programRules.condition: (#{BloodSugarTypeOption} == 'FBS'|| #{BloodSugarTypeOption} == 'RBS'|| #{BloodSugarTypeOption} == 'PPBS') && #{BloodSugarTypeUnits} == ''
programRules.description: If users selects one of: RBS FBS or PPBS then, then show the field that lets user select unit type. But keep the fields where user enters the numeric reading hidden.
programRules.name: HIDE: RBS FBS or PPBS diabetes numeric field if blood sugar unit not selected

programRules.condition: d2:hasValue(A{dob}) && d2:hasValue(A{age}) && A{age} < 40
programRules.name: HIDESECTION Risk Assessment if age < 40y

trackedEntityAttributes.formName: By tapping the checkbox you confirm that any patient data you enter has been obtained under appropriate informed consent in compliance with any applicable laws. At a minimum, this means that the patient or legal guardian consents to you entering their personal data into the app, understands what data is being collected, and agrees that they may be contacted via SMS, WhatsApp or other methods using the phone number provided in the app. Anyone authorized by you or the healthcare provider for whom you work, including third parties such as telecom companies, will have access to the patient data and information you enter here. Depending on the location, you may need to take additional steps to document consent in accordance with local laws. By using the app you confirm that the patient understands who will have access to the data obtained through this app and consents to that access.

visualizations.description:
*Patients under care*: Patients with hypertension with at least 1 visit in the past 12 months, excluding dead and 12 month lost to follow-up patients.
*Patients registered for hypertension*: Number of newly registered hypertension patients each month
*Patients registered for hypertension (cumulative)*: Total patients registered in hypertension program.

*Patients registered for diabetes (cumulative)*: Total patients registered in diabetes program.
